original: Louis IV [Louis of Overseas], crowned King of France
The Norwegian Battle of Kalvskinnet outside Nidaros. Earl Erling Skakke is killed, and the battle changes the tide of the civil war
Pope Innocent III fires Adolf I as archbishop of Cologne
Rabbi Mir of Rothenbur imprisoned in the fortress of Ensisheim, Alsace, by King Rudolf I (he died there seven years later)
Garrison under Dutch Resisteance leader Adrian van Swieten occupies Oudewater
English colonists sailed from Roanoke Island, North Carolina
Spanish Armada heavily damaged in storms in the Bay of Biscay along the Galician coast of Spain, some ships return to A Coruña for repairs
Merga Bien arrested for witchcraft in Fulda, Germany, part of Fulda witch trials. She and about 250 people later burned at the stake.
Peace of Cherasco: Charles de Gonzaga-Nevers becomes Duke of Mantua
Polish parliament selects Litouwer Michael Wisniopwiecki as king
Albany Congress held by seven British colonies & Iroquois indians
Swedish theologian and philosopher Emanuel Swedenborg reports the completion of the Second Coming of Christ in his work True Christian Religion
French Revolution: The National Assembly issues a decree abolishing the titles, orders and other privileges of the French nobility
Russian Admiral Dmitry Senyavin destroys the Ottoman fleet in the Battle of Athos during Russo-Turkish War
Battle of Seven Oaks between North West Company and Hudson's Bay Company, near Winnipeg, Manitoba
Decisive defeat of the Greek revolutionary society Philikí Etaireía by the Ottomans at Drăgăşani (in Wallachia)
New Orleans gives US government Jackson Square to be used as a mint
First officially recognized baseball game (played by Cartwright Rules) - NY Nine defeats the NY Knickerbockers 23-1 in Hoboken, New Jersey
Anaheim Post Office established
Francis Pierpont is elected provisional governor of West Virginia
Slavery is outlawed in US territories
Battle at Middleburg, Virginia, with 100+ casualties
CSS "Alabama" sunk by USS "Kearsarge" off Cherbourg, France
Skirmish at Pine Knob, Georgia
1st Belmont Stakes: Gilbert Gilpatrick aboard Ruthless wins in 3:05
Major General E. R. S. Canby removes mayor of Columbia, South Carolina
Formal opening of US Marine Hospital at Presidio in San Francisco
The Herzegovinian rebellion against the Ottoman Empire begins
Muhammad Ahmad becomes Mahdi of Sudan
MLB Baltimore Orioles outfielder Wee Willie Keeler's then-record 44 game hitting streak ends
New York Central Railroad begins building Grand Central Terminal, designed by the architectural firms of Reed & Stem (overall) and Warren &  Wetmore (exterior)
First baseball game played under lights, an exhibition between local Elks lodge teams in Cincinnati, Ohio
Father's Day is celebrated for the first time, in Spokane, Washington
Tennessee University is established (as the Tennessee Agricultural & Industrial State Normal School for Negroes), in Nashville, Tennessee
Natives Land Act, Act No 27, passed in South Africa: confines Africans to hopelessly overcrowded reserves and deprives them of rights to purchase land outside the native reserves
Hillcrest Mine Disaster: explosion at Hillcrest mine, Alberta, kills 189 men in Canada's worst mining disaster
The British Royal Family, which has had strong German ties since George I, renounces its German names and titles and adopts the name of Windsor
Opposed to dismemberment of Turkey by the Allies, Mustafa Kemal declares his Turkish Nationalist Congress, headquartered in Ankara, independent of Constantinople
Census held in Great Britain
Turks and Christians of Palestine sign a friendship treaty against Jews
Comic Strip "Moon Mullins" debuts
Paavo Nurmiruns world record 1500m of 3:52.6; 50 minutes later sets 5,000m WR 14:28.2; also runs WR 3-mile time 14:02.00 within that event in Finnish Olympic trials in Helsinki
DeFord Bailey is 1st African American to perform on Nashville's "Grand Ole Opry"
First photoelectric cell installed commercially, in West Haven, Connecticut
First concert given in San Francisco's Stern Grove
Hailstones kill 200 in Hunan Province, China
Austrian Chancellor Engelbert Dollfuss' government bans Nazi organizations
Federal Communications Commission (FCC) created
Dutch Prime Minister Hendrikus Colijn denies relation with German call-girl
LPGA Western Open Women's Golf, Beverly CC: Helen Hicks wins the first of her 2 major titles, defeating amateur Bea Barrett 6 & 5 in the final
"Olympian Flyer" express train crashes in Montana, killing 47
FIFA World Cup Final, Stade Olympique de Colombes, Paris, France: Luigi Colausig & Silvio Piola each score 2 goals as Italy beats Hungary, 4-1
Reds Johnny Vander Meer extends his string of hitless baseball innings to 21 2/3 before Debs Garms singles for Boston in 4th
Romania orders Jewish evacuation of Darabani
Paul Waner is 7th to get 3,000 baseball hits
NFL's Philadelphia Eagles & Pittsburgh Steelers merge, (dissolves on Dec 5)
Day one of the two-day Battle of the Philippine Sea, US naval forces defeat the Japanese fleet in World War II
French troops free Elba
Heavy air raid on US fleet at Guam "Turkey Shoot"
Japanese troops conquer Changsha China
El Teniente Mine accident [Smoke Tragedy] 355 men die from smoke inhalation from a fire in the Andes, Chile
First TV sports and boxing spectacular,Joe LouisKOs Billy Conn in a match broadcast across New York
First plane (F-80) to exceed 600 mph (1,004 kph) - Albert Boyd, Muroc, California
Panama and Costa Rica recognize Israel
"I've Got A Secret" debuts on CBS-TV with Garry Moore as host
Brooklyn Dodgers pitcher Carl Erskine no-hits Chicago Cubs, 5-0
Albert W Dent elected president of US National Health Council
WCSC TV channel 5 in Charleston, South Carolina (CBS) begins broadcasting
WTPA (now WHTM) TV channel 27 in Harrisburg, PA (ABC) 1st broadcast
Taz, the Tasmanian Devil, a Warner Bros. cartoon character created by Robert McKimson and Warren Foster, debuts in the Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies series in "Devil May Hare"
Philadelphia Phillies beat the Chicago Cubs, 1-0 in 15 innings; ties longest shutout in club history
1st Copa Libertadores Final, Asunción: Luis Cubilla scores 83' equaliser for Peñarol of Uruguay for 1-1 draw against Olimpia (Paraguay); win 2-1 on aggregate after taking 1st-leg 1-0 in Montevideo
"Little Egypt (Ying-Yang)" by The Coasters peaks at #23
Kuwait declares independence from the United Kingdom
US Supreme Court struck down a provision in Maryland's constitution requiring state office holders to believe in God
Charter members of Canadian Football Hall of Fame chosen
Greek government of Pipinolis forms
Two Russian space missions return to Earth
Cambuur Leeuwarden BVO soccer team forms in Leeuwarden
KYW-AM in Cleveland Ohio returns call letters to Philadelphia
50,000 participate in Solidarity Day March of Poor People's Campaign
State troopers ordered to Cairo Ill, to quell racial disturbances
Conservatives led byEdward Heathwin British parliamentary election
Russian cosmonauts Andriyan Nikolayev and Vitaly Sevastyanov return after nearly18 days in Soyuz 9, the longest crewed spaceflight at the time (eclipsed by Soyuz 11 in 1971)
The international Patent Cooperation Treaty is signed
Mayor declares state of emergency in Columbus Georgia due to racial disturbance
-29] Tropical storm Agnes upgraded to a hurricane, makes landfall in Panama City (would kill 128)
A Catholic civilian is shot dead by the Provisional Irish Republican Army in the Cracked Cup Social Club, Belfast
Hundreds of thousands of holidaymakers face flight delays and cancellations after pilots threaten to strike over hijack fears
Secretary of State for Northern Ireland William Whitelaw concedes 'special category' status, or 'political status' for paramilitary prisoners in Northern Ireland
"The Rocky Horror Show" stage production first opens in London written by Richard O'Brian and directed by Jim Sharman and starring Tim Curry
KC Royals pitcher Steve Busby throws his 2nd career no-hitter; beats Milwaukee Brewers, 2-0
Yemen Arab Republic (North Yemen) suspends constitution
US Viking 1 goes into Martian orbit after 10-month flight from Earth
Red Sox set 3 game record of 16 HRs, all against Yanks
Mali's constitution goes into effect
Battle between police and demonstrators in Capetown, 34 killed
Boeing commercial Chinook 2-rotor helicopter is certified
European Space Agency's Ariane carries 2 satellites into orbit
Heaviest known orange (2.5 kg) exhibited, Nelspruit, South Africa
India's APPLE satellite, 1st to be stabilized on 3 axes, launched
The body of God's Banker, Roberto Calvi is found hanging beneath Blackfriars Bridge in London
1st live TV appearance by Chief Justice Warren Burger (Nightline)
ETA bomb attack in Barcelona, 15 killed
Supreme Court rules school teaching evolution need not teach creation
32 divers finish cycling underwater on a standard tricycle, to complete 116.66 mi in 75 hrs 20 mins
Namphy takes control of Haitian government
World's then largest sausage completed at 13 1/8 miles long
Barry Switzer resigns as head coach of Oklahoma's football team
NY Yankee Steve Howe records his 1st major league save since 1987
Two of actressMia Farrow's daughters are arrested for shoplifting lingerie
Guardian Angel Curtis Sliwa is shot twice in NYC
Inkhata-blood bath in Boipatong South-Africa
NY Yankees 1st game in Baltimore Oriole's Camden Yards
Ernesto Samper elected president of Colombia
Tigers tie record of hitting HRs in 25th consecutive games
Prime ministers of several northern European nations participate in a ceremonial "laying of the first stone" at the Svalbard Global Seed Vault in Spitsbergen, Norway
Stanley Cup Final, PNC Arena, Raleigh, NC: Carolina Hurricanes beat Edmonton Oilers, 3-1 for a 4-3 series victory; Hurricane's first Championship
A man is beheaded for witchcraft and sorcery in Saudi Arabia
Antonis Samaras, the leader of the New Democracy party in Greece, forms a coalition government
48 people are killed by armed bandits in Zamfara State, Nigeria
The right-wing Venstre block wins the 2015 Danish general election
Bexit negotiations begin between United Kingdom and the European Union in Brussels
First full genetic study of cats published in "Nature Ecology and Evolution," reveals they were domesticated 9,000 years ago and are descended from one species, the African wildcat
Record 65.6 million people displaced around the world in 2016 reported by UN refugee agency
Russia warns the US it will target US and allied aircraft over Syria after US fighter shoots down Syrian warplane
Canada's Senate votes to legalize recreational marijuana use, first major economy to do so
England smash the highest score (481/6) in one day international cricket history in a 242 run defeat of Australia (239) at Trent Bridge
General Electric is dropped form the Dow Jones Industrial Average, the last original member from 1907
UN says over 70 million people in the world are displaced, asylum seekers or refugees around the world, their highest-ever number in 70 years
US NXIVM cult leader Keith Raniere found guilty in New York court of racketeering, sex trafficking and fraud
US Senators receive a classified briefing on possible UFO sightings by the US Navy
Columbia Records releases "Rough and Rowdy Ways", the 39th studio album by singer-songwriterBob Dylan
Colombian elects its first leftist President, former member of the M-19 guerrilla movement Gustavo Petro
French President Emmanuel Marcon's centralist coalition loses its ruling majority in the National Assembly after gains by the right and the left in legislative elections
US Center for Disease Control recommends COVID-19 vaccines for kids aged 6-months to 5 years
Heatwave with temperatures between 42C and 47C in Indian province of Uttar Pradesh, with a controversy erupting after official suggests dozens have died because of it
Historians' study commissioned by Dutch parliament finds rulers William III, William IV and William V made more than €545m profit from Dutch colonies, including through slavery in Africa and Asia
Search and rescue mission begins for the Titan submarine on an expedition to explore the wreck of the Titanic in the North Atlantic
UN adopts first-ever legally binding international treaty to govern the high seas (Biodiversity Beyond National Jurisdiction Treaty)
A law requiring classrooms display the Ten Commandments signed into law by Louisiana Governor Jeff Landry - first state to do so
At least 550 people are reported to have died on hajj pilgrimage to Mecca, Saudi Arabia, as temperatures topped 51.8 degrees C (125.2 F)
World's oldest wine, a white, revealed inside a 1st century CE Roman funeral urn mixed with a man's ashes in a mausoleum in Carmona, Spain
KingLouis IXof France decrees all Jews must wear a yellow badge in public or be fined 10 livres of silver
The Earl of Pembroke's army defeatsRobert the Bruce's Scottish army at the Battle of Methven
French KingLouis XIforms postal service
Emperor Maximilian Iand England sign the Treaty of Antwerp
Samuel de Champlainand his French army defeat the Mohawk people at the Battle of Sorel in New France, present-day Sorel-Tracy, Quebec
Russian mathematicianLeonhard Eulerleaves St. Petersburg for a position at the Berlin Academy, offered to him byFrederick the Greatof Prussia
George Washington's Continental Army troops finally leave Valley Forge, their winter encampment
Gioachino Rossini's opera "Il viaggio a Reims" (The Journey to Reims) premieres in celebration of the coronation of French King Charles X
Republican Party nominates PresidentWilliam McKinleyfor re-election, but chooses a new candidate for Vice-President,Theodore Roosevelt
Paavo Nurmiruns world record 5000m (14:28.2)
German boxerMax SchmelingKOs up-and-coming American heavyweightJoe Louisin 12 rounds at Yankee Stadium, New York
Joe McCarthyis named to manage AL All-Stars, rather than high-strungMickey Cochrane, who is very close to a nervous breakdown
Francisco Franco's Nationalist troops take Bilbao during their victorious Biscay Campaign in the Basques region during the Civil War
Second of two legendary recording sessions by Delta Blues musicianRobert Johnsonwith producer Don Law at the Vitagraph Studios, Dallas, Texas
German 7th Armoured division under command ofRommeloccupies Cherbourg
Hermann Goeringorders seizure of Dutch horses, car, buses and ships

Soviet anthropologist Mikhail Gerasimov opens the tomb of Timurid Empire founderTimurand allegedly finds an inscription stating that whoever opens the tomb shall "unleash an invader more terrible than I." Three days later, Germany invades the Soviet Union.
US PresidentFranklin Rooseveltsigns the Two Ocean Navy Expansion Act - increases the size of US Navy by 70%
Five of the "The Magnificent Eleven" photos taken byRobert Capaduring the D-Day landings at Omaha Beach, Normandy first published in "Life Magazine"
Mickey Mantlehits career HR # 100
Jerry LewisandDean Martinend their partnership after 10 years and 16 films
US Senate rejectsEisenhower's appointment of Lewis Strauss as Secretary of Commerce
Loretta Lynnrecords "I'm a Honky Tonk Girl"
Charlie Finley, changes A's manager Joe Gordon (26-33) forHank Bauer

Valentina Tereshkova, the first woman in space, returns to Earth

Civil Rights Act of 1964passes 73-27

Algerian coup under colonel Houari Boumedienne removes presidentAhmed Ben Bellafrom power
Paul McCartneyadmits that he took LSD in a statement to Independent Television News (ITN); he was tha last Beatle to experiment with the drug, but first to speak about it publicly
British Conservative government ofEdward Heathforms withMargaret Thatcherin the Cabinet
Jim Bouton's controversial baseball diary "Ball Four" is published
Carole Kingstarts a five-week run at No. 1 on the U.S. singles chart with the double A-sided single "It's Too Late / I Feel The Earth Move."
Pete Rose(Cincinnati Reds) andWillie Davis(LA Dodgers) both record 2,000th MLB career hit; Rose, a single in 4-0 win vs SF Giants; Davis, a HR in 3-0 win vs Atlanta Braves

Garfield, created byJim Davis, first appears as a comic strip
In NYC 36,211 show up to witness return ofBilly Martinas manager of the New York Yankees

MetsDwight Goodenwins his 100th game (100-37)

"Batman Returns", starringMichael Keatonas Batman,Danny DeVitoas the Penguin, andMichelle Pfeifferas Catwoman, is released
Evander HolyfieldbeatsLarry Holmesin 12 for heavyweight boxing title
NY Yankees announce agreement withDarryl Strawberry

At about 4:30 pm,Stephen Kingis hit by a car on the shoulder of Route 5, in Lovell, Maine. He would suffer numerous injuries, including a collapsed right lung, multiple fractures of his right leg, scalp laceration and a broken hip
Stanley Cup, Marine Midland Arena, Buffalo, NY: Dallas Stars edge Buffalo Sabres, 2-1 in triple overtime for 4-2 series win;Brett Hullclinches series with infamous "No Goal" ruling
Michael Schumacherwins controversial United States Formula 1 Grand Prix where only 6 of 20 cars complete the race amongst ridicule of F1 safety and tyre rules
Ringo Starr's tenth All-Starr Band debuts in concert; members include: Billy Squier, Colin Hay, Edgar Winter, Gary Wright, Hamish Stuart, and Gregg Bissonette
"Transformers: Revenge of the Fallen" directed byMichael Bayand starringShia LaBeoufandMegan Foxis released
Jerry Sloanreturns to the Utah Jazz as an adviser and scouting consultant
"Transformers: Age of Extinction" directed byMichael Bayand starringMark Wahlbergpremieres in Hong Kong. Highest grossing film of 2014 ($1.104 billion)
Felipe VIascends to the Spanish throne after the abdication of his father King Juan Carlos
American actressShannen Doherty(44) reveals that she has breast cancer
Pixar's animated film "Inside Out" is released with voices byAmy Poehler, Phyllis Smith, Lewis Black, Bill Hader and Mindy Kaling
Alex Puccioundergoes spinal fusion surgery due to a herniated disk
US ambassadorNikki Haleyannounces the US is leaving the UN Human Rights Council
First debate for a decade in Congress over reparations for slavery withDanny Gloverone of the witnesses

Australian government and companies suffer a months-long state-based cyber attack according to PMScott Morrison

Cyril Ramaphosais sworn in for a second term as President of South Africa in Pretoria, at the head of a coalition government
Russia'sVladimir Putinand North Korea'sKim Jong-unsign a pact to "provide mutual assistance in case of aggression" in Pyongyang, bringing the countries the closest since the Cold War

HMS Kite chases the steamship SS Savannah on its historic voyage across the Atlantic for 3 hours off the coast of Ireland believing it to be on fire. Unable to catch the steamship HMS Kite fires warning shots forcing it to stop and be inspected to the amazement of the British.
